claims.claim: c2patool installs on macOS via 'brew install c2patool'
Running c2patool against an asset prints a summary JSON report of its C2PA manifests; the -d / --detailed flag prints the detailed manifest data
c2patool is available as a Homebrew formula (CLI for working with C2PA manifests and media assets)
claims.raw_snippet: c2patool: stable 0.26.62 (bottled), HEAD - CLI for working with C2PA manifests and media assets

claims.claim: ExifTool installs on macOS via 'brew install exiftool'
claims.raw_snippet: exiftool: stable 13.55 (bottled) - Perl lib for reading and writing EXIF metadata

claims.claim: Content Credentials provides a free web Verify tool where you can inspect an image's provenance, hosted at contentcredentials.org
claims.raw_snippet: contentcredentials.org/verify redirects (301 Moved Permanently) to the Content Authenticity Initiative Verify tool at verify.contentauthenticity.org

claims.claim: Leading automated AI-image detectors mislead users: across a NewsGuard audit they declared authentic images to be AI-generated 13.33 percent of the time, and one tool got it wrong 40 percent of the time
claims.raw_snippet: The tools collectively declared authentic images to be AI-generated 13.33 percent of the time, and one tool got it wrong 40 percent of the time.

claims.claim: Experts called the June 2025 Iran-Israel conflict the first major conflict where generative AI is shaping the information battlefield, with game clips and AI-generated content passed off as real events
claims.raw_snippet: According to the BBC, game clips and AI-generated content are being “passed off as real events” across social media. This marks what experts call the first major conflict where generative AI is shaping the information battlefield.

claims.claim: Verification group Geoconfirmed flagged a surge of misleading media including recycled strike footage, video game clips, and deepfakes during the Iran-Israel conflict
claims.raw_snippet: verification group Geoconfirmed flagged a surge of misleading media, including recycled strike footage, video game clips, deepfakes, and “AI-generated content being passed off as real events.”

claims.claim: Pro-Iran TikTok accounts shared flight simulator clips as real airstrikes, reaching over 21 million views before removal, and fake videos of missile damage in Tel Aviv and Ben Gurion Airport circulated
claims.raw_snippet: pro-Iran TikTok accounts shared flight simulator clips as real airstrikes, reaching over 21 million views before removal. Fake videos showing missile damage in Tel Aviv and Ben Gurion Airport were shared, with some traced to Iran-linked sources.

claims.claim: Much viral war imagery is not real or current: it is either AI-generated or old footage misrepresented as new
claims.raw_snippet: Much of that viral imagery doesn't show real or current events. Often, it's generated with artificial intelligence or else it's old footage misrepresented as if it's new.

claims.claim: AI-generated content related to the Iran-Israel conflict took disinformation to an industrial level
claims.raw_snippet: AI-generated content related to the Iran-Israel conflict has taken disinformation to an industrial level

claims.claim: In April 2025 an Israeli broadcast aired a manipulated AI video; the anchor stopped it on air, saying the words were not the minister's but AI inserting messages
claims.raw_snippet: On the first sentence I said stop the video. We apologize. This is cooked… These are not Gallant's words but AI trying to insert messages about the U.S. and the Houthis.

claims.claim: The Bank of Israel warns the public about fraud by criminals impersonating representatives of banks, credit card companies, the Bank of Israel, and the Israel Police
claims.raw_snippet: Criminal elements are trying to conduct fraudulent activity with the aim of misappropriating funds from customers by impersonating representatives of banks, credit card companies, the Bank of Israel, and the Israel Police among others.

claims.claim: Israel's 105 hotline (Child Online Protection Bureau) takes reports of online harm to minors, including impersonation and AI-generated voices and videos, and is reachable by calling 105
claims.raw_snippet: The 105 hotline is a unique national call center that accepts queries and reports from the public and provides professional responses to harm, violence, and crime aimed at children and adolescents in cyberspace.

claims.claim: 119 is Israel's national cybersecurity hotline, part of the CERT run by the National Cyber Directorate, a 24/7 free service where anyone can report a cyberattack or suspicion of attack
claims.raw_snippet: 119 — yes, 911 backwards — is the cybersecurity hotline Israelis can now call if they are concerned that they are under cyberattack, making Israel possibly one of the first countries in the world to set up such a hotline. The initiative is part of the national CERT — Computer Emergency Response Team — that is based in Beersheba. The hotline ... is a 24/7 free service where anyone can report a cyberattack or suspicion of attack and get immediate help.

claims.claim: ElevenLabs offers a free AI Speech Classifier that detects whether an audio clip was created using ElevenLabs and reports the probability it is AI-generated
claims.raw_snippet: Our AI Speech Classifier lets you detect whether an audio clip was created using ElevenLabs. Upload your sample and understand the probability of it being AI generated.

claims.claim: Google SynthID embeds an imperceptible watermark into AI-generated images, audio, and video produced by Google AI models
claims.raw_snippet: SynthID embeds digital watermarks directly into AI-generated images, audio, text or video.
